description: Shared schema for design-altitude review — used by both the frame-time design-sketch review and the review-time approach pass. Distinct from finding-schema.json, which the correctness pass uses.

verdict.description: Required, even when there are no findings. Frame: is this sketch a sound, modern way to satisfy the spec — and if not, what shape would you build instead? Review: if you built this from the spec yourself, would you build it this way? Name the simpler/declarative/library-based design when one exists.

findings.description: Empty array if the shape is sound and idiomatic. Approach pass: at most the 3 highest-leverage concerns.

severity.description: How strongly the shape is wrong / how big the win.

reversibility.description: one-way = expensive to reverse (architecture, data model, public contract, a new dependency, OR a cross-cutting pattern future code will copy) → blocks, needs Thomas. two-way = locally reversible → defaults to Claude, logged for veto.

standing.description: Best-practice assessment vs. modern idiom AND the repo's own conventions. 'standard' findings are usually QUESTIONs/clarifications; the others are the flags.

locus.description: Where it lives: a design-sketch section at frame time, or a file:line / module at review time. Null for a finding about absent code (something that should exist but doesn't).

claim.description: What is structurally suboptimal / substandard, and why it matters.

alternative.description: The simpler / more declarative / more idiomatic design that would replace it.

win.description: The CONCRETE payoff that justifies the alternative — lines removed, a dependency dropped, an error path eliminated, an invariant centralized. Novelty alone ('it is newer') is not a win.
